description: The Tokyo-based exchange Mt. Gox, which handled over 70% of all Bitcoin transactions, filed for bankruptcy in February 2014 after losing 850,000 bitcoins. The collapse catalyzed a global debate on whether cryptocurrency exchanges should be subjected to mandatory government oversight or remain decentralized.

key_events.description: Mt. Gox suspends withdrawals citing technical issues
CEO Mark Karpeles admits 850k BTC are missing
Mt. Gox files for bankruptcy protection in Japan
US Senate holds hearing on Bitcoin risks

calibration_notes: Measures the shift from 'crypto-libertarian enthusiasm' to 'mainstream distrust' after a major infrastructure failure.
